title: STANDARD FOR GARI CXS 151-1989 Adopted in 1989. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2013, 2019, 2023.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR GARI CXS 151-1989 Adopted in 1989. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2013, 2019, 2023.
sections.summary: Document title and status: Standard for Gari (CXS 151-1989) adopted 1989, revised 1995, and amended in 2013, 2019 and 2023.
Section heading indicating a 2023 amendment; no substantive provisions in this heading.

sections.section: Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, amendments were made in the Annex.
sections.summary: Notice that, following decisions at the 46th Codex Alimentarius Commission (Dec 2023), amendments were made to the Annex.
Scope: this standard applies to gari intended for direct human consumption obtained from processing cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz).

Defines gari as the finished product from artisanal or industrial processing of cassava tubers involving peeling, washing, grating, fermentation, pressing, fragmentation, granulation, drying if necessary, sifting and suitable heat treatment, presented as a flour of variable granule size.

Defines the product: gari is the finished product obtained from processing cassava tubers (Manihot esculenta Crantz) by steps including peeling, washing, grating, fermentation, pressing, fragmentation, granulation, optional drying, sifting and heat treatment, presented as flour of variable granule size.

General quality requirements: gari shall be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours, odours and living insects, and free from filth (including dead insects) in amounts that may pose a health hazard.

Specifies moisture content requirements: maximum 12.0% m/m for gari, with the note that lower moisture limits may be required for certain destinations depending on climate, transport duration and storage.
Sets moisture content maximum at 12.0% m/m and notes that lower limits may be required for particular destinations based on climate, transport duration and storage conditions.
Requires that, according to good manufacturing practices, gari shall be practically free from extraneous matter.

Contaminants requirements: the product must comply with maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.
Requires compliance with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
Requires compliance with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticide residues in the product.

Hygiene provisions: recommends preparing and handling gari per the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex codes, requires the product to be, to the extent possible, free from objectionable matter and, when tested, free from microorganisms, parasites and microbial-origin substances in amounts that may represent a health hazard.

sections.section: 5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) 2  and other codes of practice recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission which are relevant to this product.
sections.summary: Recommends that gari be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex codes of practice.

sections.section: 5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
sections.summary: States the obligation that, as far as practicable under good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.

sections.section: 5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
sections.summary: Requires that, when tested by appropriate sampling and examination methods, the product be free from microorganisms, parasites, and microbial-origin substances in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.

Sets packaging requirements for gari: containers must protect hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities and be made of safe materials that do not impart toxic substances or undesirable odours/flavours; sacks must be clean, sturdy and securely closed; and defines 'suitable heat treatment' (toasting, grilling or other cooking producing characteristic organoleptic properties with partial starch gelatinization and dehydration).

sections.section: 6.1 Gari  shall  be  packaged  in  containers  which  will  safeguard  the  hygienic,  nutritional,  technological,  and organoleptic qualities of the product.
sections.summary: Requires that gari be packaged in containers that safeguard the product's hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities.

sections.section: 6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.
sections.summary: Mandates that containers and packaging materials be made of substances safe and suitable for intended use and not impart toxic substances or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.

sections.section: i Suitable heat treatment means toasting, grilling or any other method of cooking capable of producing the characteristic organoleptic properties of the product. During the heat treatment, there is a partial gelatinization of the starch and the dehydration of gari grains.
sections.summary: Defines 'suitable heat treatment' as toasting, grilling or other cooking that produces the product's characteristic organoleptic properties and causes partial starch gelatinization and dehydration of gari grains.

sections.section: 6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.
sections.summary: Specifies that when packaged in sacks the product must use sacks that are clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

States that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods, the standard contains specific labelling provisions for gari (including the product name and rules for non-retail container information).
Requires that the name shown on the label of the product shall be 'gari'.

Provides non-retail container labelling rules: information may be on the container or accompanying documents, but the product name, lot identification and the manufacturer/packer name and address must appear on the container (these may be replaced by an identification mark if clearly identifiable with accompanying documents).
Requires that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance follow the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions.

Annex provides analytical limits and classification criteria for gari: total acidity 0.6%–1% (as lactic acid), crude fibre max 2%, ash max 2.75%; enrichment, food additives and optional ingredients must conform to legislation of the country of sale; and specifies sieve-based classification requirements (minimum/maximum percent by weight passing specified sieve sizes) for extra-fine, fine, medium, coarse and unclassified gari.
